cards.prompt: An async IAsyncEnumerable<int> with yield return, consumed via await foreach, prints the squares 1 4 9
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Text; using System.Threading.Tasks; async IAsyncEnumerable<int> Nums(){ for(int i=1;i<=3;i++){ await Task.Delay(1); yield return i*i; } } var sb = new StringBuilder(); await foreach (var n in Nums()) sb.Append(n).Append(" "); Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString().Trim());

cards.prompt: IAsyncEnumerator<T>.MoveNextAsync returns ValueTask<bool>, and the enumerator is IAsyncDisposable
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Threading.Tasks; using System.Reflection; bool vt = typeof(IAsyncEnumerator<int>).GetMethod("MoveNextAsync").ReturnType == typeof(ValueTask<bool>); bool disp = typeof(IAsyncDisposable).IsAssignableFrom(typeof(IAsyncEnumerator<int>)); Console.WriteLine($"moveNextIsValueTaskBool={vt} enumeratorIsAsyncDisposable={disp}");

cards.prompt: An async stream is lazy/on-demand: you can break out of an infinite one after 3 elements (took=3 vals=012)
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Text; using System.Threading.Tasks; async IAsyncEnumerable<int> Infinite(){ int i=0; while(true){ await Task.Delay(1); yield return i++; } } int taken=0; var sb=new StringBuilder(); await foreach (var n in Infinite()){ sb.Append(n); if(++taken==3) break; } Console.WriteLine($"took={taken} vals={sb}");
